tech.title: First controlled nuclear chain reaction
tech.description: Enrico Fermi and his team achieve the first self-sustaining nuclear chain reaction at Chicago Pile-1, proving that controlled nuclear fission is possible.

tech.title: U.S. Environmental Protection Agency begins operations
tech.description: The new Environmental Protection Agency opens its doors, centralizing many federal environmental programs under one agency.

tech.title: SOHO solar observatory launched
tech.description: NASA and ESA launch the Solar and Heliospheric Observatory (SOHO) to continuously monitor the Sun and improve understanding of space weather.

history.title: Napoleon crowned Emperor of the French
history.description: Napoleon Bonaparte crowns himself Emperor of the French at Notre-Dame Cathedral in Paris, ending the First Republic and beginning the First French Empire.

history.title: Battle of Austerlitz
history.description: Napoleon’s army decisively defeats Russian and Austrian forces at Austerlitz, one of his most famous military victories.

history.title: Monroe Doctrine announced
history.description: U.S. President James Monroe sets out the Monroe Doctrine, warning European powers not to interfere in the Western Hemisphere.

history.title: United Arab Emirates is formed
history.description: Six emirates on the Persian Gulf formally unite to create the United Arab Emirates; Ras Al Khaimah joins the federation later.

world_events.title: Bhopal gas disaster
world_events.description: A toxic gas leak from a Union Carbide pesticide plant in Bhopal, India, kills thousands of people and becomes one of the world’s deadliest industrial disasters.

world_events.title: Benazir Bhutto becomes Pakistan’s prime minister
world_events.description: Benazir Bhutto is sworn in as Prime Minister of Pakistan, becoming the first woman to lead the government of a Muslim-majority country.

world_events.title: Pablo Escobar killed in Medellín
world_events.description: Colombian drug lord Pablo Escobar is tracked down and killed by police in Medellín, marking a major blow to the Medellín Cartel.

world_events.title: Enron files for bankruptcy
world_events.description: Energy giant Enron files for Chapter 11 bankruptcy after an accounting fraud scandal, becoming one of the largest corporate collapses in U.S. history at the time.

world_events.title: San Bernardino mass shooting
world_events.description: A married couple opens fire at a holiday gathering in San Bernardino, California, killing 14 people and wounding many others before dying in a shootout with police.

births.title: Birth of Georges Seurat
births.description: French painter known for pioneering pointillism, whose works include "A Sunday Afternoon on the Island of La Grande Jatte."

births.title: Birth of Gianni Versace
births.description: Italian fashion designer who founded the Versace brand and became one of the most influential designers of the late 20th century.

births.title: Birth of Britney Spears
births.description: American pop singer and entertainer who becomes one of the best-selling music artists of the late 1990s and 2000s.

births.title: Birth of Aaron Rodgers
births.description: American football quarterback who wins multiple NFL MVP awards and a Super Bowl with the Green Bay Packers.

deaths.title: Death of Hernán Cortés
deaths.description: Spanish conquistador who led the expedition that overthrew the Aztec Empire and brought large parts of Mexico under Spanish rule.

deaths.title: Execution of John Brown
deaths.description: American abolitionist John Brown is hanged for leading the raid on Harpers Ferry in an attempt to spark a slave uprising.

deaths.title: Death of Desi Arnaz
deaths.description: Cuban-American actor, musician, and producer best known for co-starring in and producing the TV series "I Love Lucy."
